seoTitle: Parkes vs Parkesbourne — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Parkes and Parkesbourne. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Parkes and Parkesbourne.
heading: Parkes vs Parkesbourne — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Parkes, New South Wales and Parkesbourne, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Parkesbourne has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Parkes (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Parkes or Parkesbourne safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Parkes has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Parkesbourne scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Parkes scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Parkesbourne scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Parkes or Parkesbourne?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Parkesbourne has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
